patch.value: Backfill terminal resolution evidence without placeholder metadata
Backfill historical terminal-item resolution/expected/actual metadata and residual rationale without adding meaningless placeholders.

All active item acceptance criteria and estimates remain filled or explicitly waived; terminal items flagged by validate_resolution_missing_fields are reduced or grouped into a residual list with rationale; no placeholder resolution text is added; pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift evidence is attached after the pass.

value.author: codex-pm-ecosystem-audit-20260625
value.text: Duplicate check: status-all searches for terminal resolution metadata backfill and active planning metadata found closed governance tooling items pm-yq7m, pm-fryg, pm-wbak-class work, and pm-6bz1, but no open execution item for historical backfill.

author: codex-ecosystem-auditor
message: Reparent open terminal metadata backlog under living roadmap after audit plan close

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Scope addition from 2026-07-04 backlog audit (pm-y904): duplicate issue-code pair GH-209 (pm-1byt canonical, pm-25se follow-up) involves two CLOSED items, so per the changelog updated_at invariant it must be resolved in this careful backfill pass (set duplicate_of on pm-25se or retitle) together with the changelog regen, not in a create-only audit.

patch.value:
## Context
pm validate reports validate_resolution_missing_fields:268 and validate_metadata_missing_close_reason:18 — historical terminal items closed before the structured-resolution era (resolution/expected_result/actual_result via pm update, close -m/-r/-d from PR#230) lack that evidence.

## Rules of engagement
- NO placeholders: only backfill where real evidence exists (item history, linked PR/commit, session memory, CHANGELOG entry). An honest empty beats "done: done".
- Changelog drag trap: touching CLOSED items updates updated_at, which pm-changelog uses for release bucketing — a bulk backfill would drag old items into the current [Unreleased]. Do the backfill in a dedicated tracker-only pass where the changelog is regenerated and reviewed in the same commit (same protocol as pm-pivf), or teach pm-changelog to bucket by closed_at first.
- Work oldest→newest in small batches; record per-batch counts (missing_fields before/after) as comments here.

## Definition of done
validate_resolution_missing_fields reduced to only items with genuinely unrecoverable evidence (documented count); missing_close_reason:18 → 0; no unintended [Unreleased] churn in the same commit's regenerated changelog.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Re-parent under new Governance/validation capability epic pm-33cw (was hanging directly off milestone pm-doxj)

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Audit evidence 2026-07-04 continuation: pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift --json is ok:true for history_drift across 1506 checked items and warns only validate_resolution_missing_fields:268 across closed historical items. This confirms pm-e9zh remains the canonical open remediation for terminal resolution/expected/actual backfill; no duplicate item created. The earlier list --filter-resolution-missing projection showed 198 closed rows because it counts a narrower filter than validate resolution details. Keep the existing no-placeholder and changelog-drag rules.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Live validation handoff from pm-lodl/pm-8778 lane: pm validate --check-resolution --check-history-drift --json on 2026-07-05 returned ok:true with history_drift ok across 1536 checked items and the pre-existing validate_resolution_missing_fields:268 warning. This confirms pm-e9zh remains the canonical open remediation for historical terminal resolution metadata; no duplicate item created from this lane.
